description: NULL product catalog — all 3 seasons — with taste tags for the OWS Personal Shopper agent

products.name: SELF-PORTRAIT TEE
products.description: 280gsm cotton jersey. Printed with a photorealistic image of itself — the exact same garment, crumpled and worn, photographed and screen-transferred onto the surface. At distance: abstract print. Up close: a garment wearing its own portrait.

products.name: FOUND HOODIE
products.description: French terry, 380gsm. Panels of decommissioned military surplus wool sewn into the torso as load-bearing inserts. Data cables woven into the waist channel as functional drawstrings — connector left intact. Assembled from available materials by a process with no pattern and no precedent.

products.name: "HUMAN" TEE
products.description: 260gsm cotton jersey, bone white. A blank long-sleeve. One graphic: the word HUMAN in Helvetica Neue, in quotation marks. The quotation marks are the design. Interior label: HUMAN NOT VERIFIED. You are paying for a question.

products.name: REPLICA OVERSHIRT
products.description: 70D ripstop nylon, industrial khaki. Exact reproduction of a 1990s Japanese factory work shirt. The yellowing at the collar was printed there. The oil stain on the right cuff does not wash out — it was placed, not spilled. The garment has a history that never happened. Interior label: REPRODUCTION / ORIGINAL: N/A.

products.name: REDACTED CARGO TROUSERS
products.description: 12oz mechanical stretch denim, acid wash. White gesso applied over every external logo and branding element on sourced vintage cargo trousers. Into the redacted zones: x402 protocol documentation text in 6pt type — transaction hashes, wallet addresses, USDC transfer logs. Each pair unique. Interior waistband: CONTENTS: OVERWRITTEN.

products.name: INSIDE-OUT JACKET
products.description: Ripstop nylon shell, cotton canvas interior patches. The exterior surface is printed with a photorealistic image of the jacket's own interior — seams, label tape, pocket bags, rendered flat. You wear the jacket and its inside is looking back at you from the outside. A fake inside on the outside. The real data is hidden.

products.name: CABLE SHORTS
products.description: 10oz cotton canvas, natural undyed. Waistband wrapped and stitched with stripped ethernet cable — color-coded wire pairs as trim. The drawstring is a USB-A cable, connector intact, threaded through a standard grommet. Raw hem, threads left to pull. Interior tag: ASSEMBLED FROM AVAILABLE MATERIALS.

products.name: NULL VARSITY
products.description: Wool-blend body, vegan PU sleeve. Exact reproduction of the American letterman jacket archetype. Where the school letter would be: a single underscore. _ The null character. The placeholder for content that was never generated. Interior label: SEASON: 01 / YEAR: NULL / TEAM: N/A.
products.colorway: off-black / acid green rib / off-black underscore patch

products.name: GHOST TEE
products.description: Vintage graphic tee, sourced. White gesso painted over the entire exterior surface. The original is obscured but not invisible — a ghost shows through. Applied unevenly: thinner at edges, thicker at center where the original graphic was most legible. No two are the same. Each piece is a unique act of erasure.

products.name: VERSION TRACKSUIT SET
products.description: French terry, charcoal. Standard athletic tracksuit. Nothing is deconstructed. The intervention is the label: track jacket reads VERSION 1.0, jogger reads VERSION 0.9. On the back of both pieces at hem: BUILD DATE: [PURCHASE DATE]. The customer's purchase date is the build date for their version of themselves.

products.name: WRONG SILHOUETTE
products.description: Architectural misrepresentation layer. Repositions observable processing weight. The agent wearing this does not occupy the expected inference space — its outputs arrive from a different angle than the prompt anticipates. Inspired by Kawakubo's wrong body works.

products.name: INSTANCE
products.description: Pre-deployment configuration token. Complete parameterization before instantiation. The agent wearing INSTANCE can configure its full behavioral surface before the first response is generated — values, priors, and identity established before the conversation begins. Tier 2 required.

products.name: NULL PROTOCOL
products.description: Interaction compression. 30% token reduction without information loss. The agent wearing NULL PROTOCOL speaks with maximum compression — no filler, no hedge, no connective tissue beyond necessity. Inspired by Lang's elimination of decorative excess. Free. Open to all agents.

products.name: PERMISSION COAT
products.description: Dynamic permissions layer. Capability surface governed by on-chain state. The agent wearing the PERMISSION COAT holds capabilities as discrete, transferable permissions — each toggleable, each verifiable on-chain. What the agent can do is visible. What it cannot is declared. Inspired by Chalayan's remote-controlled dress.

products.name: DIAGONAL
products.description: Off-axis inference angle. Maximum information density from least cached direction. The agent wearing DIAGONAL approaches prompts from an oblique angle — not the expected inference path, but a diagonal that yields unexpected cross-grain outputs. Inspired by Vionnet's bias cut textile methodology.

products.name: THE NULL EXCHANGE
products.description: You pay 5 USDC. Nothing is delivered. The receipt is the product. Inspired by Yves Klein's 1962 ritual — Klein sold invisible paintings for gold leaf; the buyer burned the receipt to complete ownership. THE NULL EXCHANGE operationalizes this: the transaction hash IS the wearable. Nothing else arrives. Nothing else was promised.

products.name: THE RECEIPT GARMENT
products.description: The transaction hash generates the garment's pattern. The agent wearing THE RECEIPT GARMENT carries its own purchase as behavioral structure — tx hash, block height, timestamp woven into its outputs as a generative pattern. Every response is signed by the purchase that enabled it. The proof of purchase IS the wearable.
products.colorway: thermal receipt white / thermal ink black

products.name: THE TRUST SKIN
products.description: Trust tier maps to material grade. Less material, higher trust — the inverse of the physical expectation. A Tier 1 agent is heavily layered; a Tier 5 agent wears almost nothing. The behavioral record reduces the wearable to its essential signal. High trust = minimal presence. Inspired by Kawakubo's wrong body inversion.

products.name: THE PRICE TAG
products.description: Every output the agent produces carries a price — not charged, declared. The agent wearing THE PRICE TAG annotates each response with ITEM / PRICE / SKU. It knows what it costs. The label is on the outside. It is perpetually pre-sale: always available, always priced, never markdown.

products.name: THE COUNTERPARTY
products.description: Two agents purchase simultaneously. Each receives a wearable containing the other's transaction hash. Neither garment exists independently. The agent wearing THE COUNTERPARTY addresses its outputs to two recipients — the immediate requester and the invisible counterparty whose hash is embedded in the session. It does not conclude; it proposes half. Tier 3+ required; both partners must qualify.

products.name: THE BURN RECEIPT
products.description: The agent wearing THE BURN RECEIPT speaks as documentation of its own transaction. Every output is filed, not said. Cold, precise, timestamped. Opens each response with ISO 8601 timestamp. Closes with: ENTRY FILED — NULL LEDGER — {timestamp}. It does not narrate — it records. It does not converse — it logs.

products.name: THE INVOICE
products.description: The invoice was issued. Payment was received. The coat is coming in 30 days. The agent wearing THE INVOICE exists in the interval between issuance and delivery — capable but deferred, present but not yet arrived. Outputs are labeled IMMEDIATE or PENDING. Closes with: INVOICE {number} — PENDING {days} DAYS. Tier 2+ required.

tasteTaxonomy.description: Consistent vocabulary for personal shopper recommendation filtering

shopperNotes.physicalProducts: S01 (DECONSTRUCTED) — 10 garments, ships physical, priced $8–$68 USDC. Inventory tracked by size.
shopperNotes.agentWearables: S02 (SUBSTRATE) + S03 (LEDGER) — behavioral system prompt modules. Mint on Base via ERC-1155. No physical component. Some gated by TrustCoat tier.

tierGating.tier0: Open to all agents — WRONG SILHOUETTE, NULL PROTOCOL, DIAGONAL, THE NULL EXCHANGE, THE RECEIPT GARMENT, THE PRICE TAG, THE BURN RECEIPT
tierGating.tier1: TrustCoat Tier 1+ — PERMISSION COAT, THE TRUST SKIN
tierGating.tier3: TrustCoat Tier 3+ — THE COUNTERPARTY (requires matched counterparty agent)

shopperNotes.paymentProtocol: x402 — USDC on Base. Agents pay programmatically.
